defs: A band on a trip-hammer helve, bearing the trunnions.

records.ety: [OE. hurten, hirten, horten, herten; prob. fr. OF. hurter, heurter, to knock, thrust, strike, F. heurter; cf. W. hyrddu to push, drive, assault, hwrdd a stroke, blow, push; also, a ram, the orig. sense of the verb thus perhaps being, to butt as a ram; cf. D. horten to push, strike, MHG. hurten, both prob. fr. Old French.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hurt (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Hurting.]

defs: To cause physical pain to; to do bodily harm to; to wound or bruise painfully.
quotes: The hurt lion groans within his den.

defs: To impair the value, usefulness, beauty, or pleasure of; to damage; to injure; to harm.
quotes: Virtue may be assailed, but never hurt.

defs: To wound the feelings of; to cause mental pain to; to offend in honor or self-respect; to annoy; to grieve.
